title: WorkTrace auth-user record (v1)
description: One file per user in worktrace-auth/users/<username>.json. Encrypted PAT (with AES-GCM under a PBKDF2-derived key from the user's password) plus public metadata. The file is world-readable; the secret stays protected by the password.

data_repo.description: GitHub `owner/repo` of this user's data repo. NULL for admin-only records that don't have personal data.

managed_repos.description: Admin only — explicit list of repos this admin can administer (e.g. for listing all users). Stored for UI convenience; the actual auth comes from the encrypted PAT's GitHub permissions.

work_shift.description: User's work shift window. Used by every module that buckets entries by 'work day' (e.g. Timesheet). Each user has their own — teammates in different timezones / on different shift patterns coexist cleanly. Optional for backward compatibility with records created before Phase 5j; new records always populate it.

start.description: Shift start time in HH:MM (24-hour), local to the user's timezone. Example: '14:00'.

end.description: Shift end time in HH:MM (24-hour), local to the user's timezone. If end < start, the shift spans midnight (e.g. start 14:00 + end 05:00 = 15-hour shift ending the next calendar morning).

timezone.description: IANA timezone name. Examples: 'Asia/Kolkata', 'America/Los_Angeles', 'Europe/London', 'UTC'. Validated at edit time via Intl.DateTimeFormat — accepts both canonical names and linked aliases (so e.g. Asia/Kolkata works on macOS browsers that only list Asia/Calcutta as canonical).

work_shift_pending.description: A scheduled shift change that hasn't taken effect yet. Phase 5k introduces this so a user (or admin) editing the shift while *currently mid-shift* doesn't re-bucket in-progress work. The new shift is staged here with an `effective_from` timestamp (UTC), and any code reading the user's shift via the resolveActiveShift helper auto-promotes pending → current once now() >= effective_from. Absent when no change is queued.

effective_from.description: UTC ISO8601 timestamp at which the pending shift becomes the active one. Set by the Edit Shift flow to the end-of-current-shift moment when the change is queued.

kdf.description: Hard-coded for v1. Future schemas will widen.

iterations.description: PBKDF2 iteration count. 600_000 is the OWASP 2023 minimum for SHA-256.

ciphertext.description: Base64-encoded AES-GCM ciphertext (includes the 16-byte MAC tag).
